quote: In the corpus the form returns 1,977 tokens at rank 108 — the highest frequency among the modal auxiliaries
issue: The specific token count and rank are given without any attached citation or source. Neither the preceding reference tags (line 15) nor any nearby tag support this quantitative claim.
suggestion: Add a citation for the corpus data, e.g., <Ref k="icls2024" /> or a note indicating the corpus source.

quote: Nakagawa documents all three at 2024: 435, §14.1.2 (exx.966–969); Satō at 2008: 85–86.
issue: The citation format mixes plain text with page references, unlike the rest of the chapter which uses <Ref> tags. It should be formatted as 'Nakagawa (2024: 435, §14.1.2)' and 'Satō (2008: 85–86)' or use <Ref> tags.
suggestion: Replace with proper parenthetical citations: 'Nakagawa (2024: 435, §14.1.2); Satō (2008: 85–86)' or insert <Ref k="nakagawa2024" p="435" /> and <Ref k="sato2008" p="85-86" />.

quote: iteki … somo eci=ki nankor na 'you had better not not do it'
issue: The translation 'you had better not not do it' is awkward and potentially confusing. The intended meaning is a double negative (do not not do it), but a simpler rendering like 'you had better do it' would be clearer.
suggestion: Simplify to 'you had better do it' or add a clarifying note about the double negation.

quote: the claim that they carry the stated meanings rests on Nakagawa's documented paradigm ‹corpus-suggested›
issue: The epistemic tag '‹corpus-suggested›' is used for claims about forms (oasi, etokus, etokoyki) for which no corpus examples are available (as stated in lines 287-288). The grading may be inflated; '‹speculative›' or '‹original-needs-review›' would be more appropriate.
suggestion: Change the tag to '‹speculative›' to reflect the lack of corpus support for these forms.
